name: Emer
description: Emer is a central female figure in Irish mythology, renowned for her wisdom, beauty, and unwavering loyalty. She is best known as the wife of the legendary hero Cú Chulainn. Emer possessed the 'six gifts of womanhood': beauty, a gentle voice, sweet words, wisdom, chastity, and skill in needlework. Her lineage is tied to nobility; she was the daughter of Forgall Monach, a cunning and formidable chieftain. Cú Chulainn had to overcome numerous trials set by Forgall to prove his worthiness as a suitor. Despite Forgall's attempts to prevent the marriage, Cú Chulainn eventually won Emer's hand, demonstrating his unparalleled strength and courage. Their marriage was a testament to the union of physical prowess and intellectual brilliance. Emer's role extends beyond that of a wife; she served as a counselor and advisor to Cú Chulainn, often providing him with strategic insights and moral guidance. She faced immense challenges and tragedies throughout her life, including Cú Chulainn's numerous affairs and his eventual death. Her steadfast love and dedication to her husband, even in the face of infidelity and loss, solidify her position as a symbol of unwavering commitment and enduring love in Irish mythology. Emer’s grief after Cú Chulainn's death is a poignant example of her deep affection and the profound impact of his loss on her life and the realm. She embodies the ideal of a strong, intelligent, and devoted woman in ancient Irish society.
subtitle: Wise, beautiful wife of Cú Chulainn
achievements: Providing wise counsel to Cú Chulainn
Embodying the six gifts of womanhood
Serving as a symbol of enduring love

keyMyths.title: The Wooing of Emer
keyMyths.description: Cú Chulainn woos Emer, but her father, Forgall Monach, opposes the match. Cú Chulainn must prove his worth through trials and ultimately abducts her.

keyMyths.title: Emer's Wisdom
keyMyths.description: Emer is known for her wisdom and often advises Cú Chulainn on matters of strategy and state. Her intelligence is highly valued in the Ulster Cycle.
keyMyths.source: Various tales within the Ulster Cycle

keyMyths.title: Emer's Grief
keyMyths.description: After Cú Chulainn's death, Emer is overcome with grief. Her lamentations highlight the depth of their love and the tragedy of his demise.

sources.author: Thomas Kinsella
sources.description: A modern translation of the Táin Bó Cúailnge, featuring Emer as Cú Chulainn's wife.
sources.text: A modern translation of the Táin Bó Cúailnge, featuring Emer as Cú Chulainn's wife.

sources.author: Jeffrey Gantz
sources.description: A collection of early Irish myths, including tales of Cú Chulainn and Emer.
sources.text: A collection of early Irish myths, including tales of Cú Chulainn and Emer.
